message:
feat(engine): flow-qualified declassification authorization matching

Replace the naive first-non-empty-string lookup in FlowEnvelope's
declassification obligation resolution with structured authorization
ref parsing. Authorized declassification entries now use the format
`<sink_clearance>:<source_label>:<obligation_id>` (e.g.
`sealed_sink:secret:obl-sealed-secret`), and the new
matching_authorized_declassification() method validates that both the
source label and sink clearance match before extracting the obligation.

This prevents false authorization when an envelope carries a
declassification ref for a different source/sink pair — previously any
non-empty string would match regardless of the flow direction.

- Add matching_authorized_declassification() with structured parsing
- Update existing test fixtures to use flow-qualified ref format
- Fix top-secret sealed-sink test to assert correct obligation ID
- Add new test: flow_assessment_ignores_non_matching_sealed_sink_authorization_refs
  verifying that a secret-only ref does not authorize a top-secret flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
